findings.description: All regular tile sources are 512x512 or 1024x1024, not the required 128x128 or 2x 256x256 tile canvas.
findings.repair_suggestion: Re-export or regenerate regular tiles to the accepted tile canvas size, preserving readable centered silhouettes.

findings.description: Several regular tiles include visible generated measurement labels or guide text inside the artwork, such as red size marks and bleed labels.
findings.repair_suggestion: Regenerate affected tiles with explicit negative prompt for labels, measurement guides, UI annotations, and text.

findings.description: Several ch1 regular tiles read more saturated and contrasty than the requested s0 muted warm stage.
findings.repair_suggestion: Apply palette correction toward 8-15% saturation and 42-48% brightness, or regenerate with stricter ch1/s0 guidance.

findings.description: All special tile frame_01 sources are 512x512, not the required 128x128 or 2x 256x256 tile canvas.
findings.repair_suggestion: Re-export special blocks to the accepted tile canvas while retaining the visible mechanic markers.

findings.description: Ritsu changes face shape, hair shape, age read, and costume details across emotions; Umeko changes age, headwear, face proportions, and costume details across the set.
findings.repair_suggestion: Regenerate expression variants from locked character sheets with fixed hair, clothing, face proportions, and signature costume colors.

findings.description: Several react_jump assets read as ordinary surprise, neutral, or happy busts rather than energetic whole-body reaction poses.
findings.repair_suggestion: Regenerate react_jump variants with clear upward motion, raised shoulders or limbs, and stronger pose energy while keeping identity locked.

findings.description: Multiple expression assets contain baked punctuation, color-code text, character labels, or Japanese text inside the artwork.
findings.repair_suggestion: Regenerate with text, punctuation, captions, labels, and color-code strings prohibited.

findings.description: Hinata happy portrait reads as a different character than Hinata default and the Hinata expression set: darker hair, different face design, and different age read.
findings.repair_suggestion: Regenerate from the Hinata reference identity with sakura-pink costume, brown tied hair, and matching face proportions.

findings.description: The two 512x512 portraits are visually not equivalent to the 1024x1024 group. Hinata happy has a noticeably different crop and identity; Fuyuko react_jump includes a scene background and baked Japanese text.
findings.repair_suggestion: Re-export or regenerate both portraits at 1024x1024 with the same portrait framing rules and no baked text or scene background.

findings.description: The Gicho frame set changes body size, face design, crop, posture, stripe pattern, and collar rendering across adjacent frames and animation sets.
findings.repair_suggestion: Regenerate animation frames from one locked model sheet or draw-over base pose, changing only the intended motion elements per frame.

findings.description: Idle f04 is a side-facing heavier pose while f01 is a front-facing seated pose, so f04 to f01 would visibly pop rather than loop.
findings.repair_suggestion: Rebuild idle as a subtle breathing cycle around one base pose, with f04 returning close to f01.

findings.description: Several adjacent frames jump between full-body, cropped bust, side view, standing, and lying compositions rather than advancing smoothly.
findings.repair_suggestion: Normalize canvas anchor, subject scale, and body orientation before generating frame-to-frame motion.

findings.description: Several animation frames contain baked labels, frame numbers, color codes, or title text, making them unusable as clean animation frames.
findings.repair_suggestion: Regenerate or paint out labels and add negative prompt coverage for titles, numbers, frame labels, and color codes.

findings.description: The representative assets share a watercolor intent, but the tile rendering is more saturated and glossy while the character assets use cleaner anime line art. The group is close but not yet fully coherent.
findings.repair_suggestion: Unify palette and texture treatment across tiles and characters, especially by muting ch1 tiles and adding the same paper/watercolor surface treatment across families.

batch_summaries.notes: Silhouettes are mostly distinguishable at 128px and still broadly readable at 32px, but tile dimensions and baked guide artifacts prevent acceptance.
Mechanic markers are visually present and distinct; dimensions fail the tile canvas requirement.
Most characters are recognizable, but Ritsu and Umeko drift materially across emotions; several assets include baked text or weak react_jump reads.
Most portraits show face and shoulders, but Hinata happy mismatches identity and the 512x512 portraits are not visually equivalent to the rest.
Wave motion is visible, but frame-to-frame identity, scale, orientation, and idle loop continuity fail animation acceptance.
The families have a broadly compatible watercolor direction, but palette and line-rendering differences are visible in the representative sheet.
